message:
test(pthread): MR signal/broadcast never block on condvar contract (bd-nqoa)

Introduces proptest scaffolding in pthread::cond::tests (property config
helper and any_state/any_op strategies) and adds the first metamorphic
proptest: prop_signal_broadcast_never_block.

POSIX requires pthread_cond_signal and pthread_cond_broadcast to return
immediately. The MR sweeps the full CondvarContractState × has_waiters
space and asserts outcome.blocks == false for both Signal and Broadcast.
This guards against any future refactor that threads the wakeup path
through the blocking Wait arm of the state machine.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-06re","title":"ssignal_and_gsignal_deliver_signal test fails (SIGNAL_HIT is 0)","description":"The `ssignal_and_gsignal_deliver_signal` test in `unistd_abi_test.rs` fails because the signal handler installed by `ssignal` is not observing `SIGUSR1` when raised via `gsignal`. The `gsignal` call returns 0 (success), but `SIGNAL_HIT.load()` remains 0 instead of 10. This indicates a bug in the signal trampoline, `sysv_signal`'s translation, or signal delivery latency/blocking.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:51:44.023709665Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:57:39.557011794Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:57:39.555064159Z","close_reason":"Current tree already fixed: ssignal/gsignal delivery and invalid-signal parity tests pass via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id": "bd-3v3", "title": "Harness: asupersync deterministic orchestration for conformance + runtime_math scenarios", "description": "Integrate /dp/asupersync into glibc-rs-harness runner.

Scope:
- Deterministic scheduling, traceability ids, structured results.
- Orchestrate scenario runs that exercise runtime_math controllers.

Acceptance criteria:
- Harness runner uses asupersync primitives for reproducible runs and evidence logging.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "CobaltForge", "created_at": "2026-02-09T21:35:42.149982753Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-10T05:21:29.149784585Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-10T05:21:29.149765789Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "comments": [{"id": 1022, "issue_id": "bd-3v3", "author": "CobaltForge", "text": "Implemented asupersync-backed deterministic conformance orchestration in glibc-rs-harness.

- Added `glibc_rs_harness::asupersync_orchestrator` (feature `asupersync-tooling`) to run fixture verification deterministically (stable fixture-set + case ordering), assign stable `trace_id`s, and emit structured evidence via `asupersync_conformance::{SuiteResult, TestResult, Checkpoint, ConformanceTestLogger}`.
- Fixed harness runner bug where fixture cases with `mode=both` were executed with mode=both (unsupported) instead of the active runner mode.
- `harness verify` now sorts fixture JSON paths deterministically and uses the orchestrator when `asupersync-tooling` is enabled; when `--report` is provided it additionally writes `<report>.suite.json` via `asupersync_conformance::write_json_report`.

Quality gates: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo check --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo test --all-targets` all pass.
", "created_at": "2026-02-10T05:21:20Z"}]}
 {"id": "bd-3vtx", "title": "bd-1rf subtask: hosts files lookup subset implementation + explicit scope boundaries", "description": "Background:
- hosts lookups are a practical dependency for many workloads and must be deterministic in files-backend scope.

Goal:
- Implement hosts files lookup subset with explicit scope boundaries and error semantics.

Deliverables:
1) Deterministic hosts parsing and lookup.
2) API mapping for supported resolver-facing calls in files scope.
3) Explicit non-goals for networked NSS backends.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Covered host lookup fixtures pass.
- Out-of-scope behavior is explicit and documented.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for IPv4/IPv6 and malformed hosts entries.
- Structured logs: trace_id, query, family, result_source, outcome, timing.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.", "notes": "### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "CloudyRobin", "created_at": "2026-02-12T15:01:53.995225917Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-15T19:03:24.895427810Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-15T19:02:54.796760654Z", "close_reason": "Implemented and verified (hosts files subset)", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "implementation", "nss", "resolver", "testing"], "comments": [{"id": 1023, "issue_id": "bd-3vtx", "author": "CloudyRobin", "text": "Implementation + verification evidence for `bd-3vtx`:

Implemented scope (files backend only; explicit non-goal for DNS/networked NSS fallback):
- `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/resolv/mod.rs`
  - added `getaddrinfo_with_hosts(...)`
  - retained numeric resolution path
  - added deterministic hosts-content lookup path and family filtering
  - explicit no-network fallback behavior (`EAI_NONAME` when unresolved in files subset)
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/resolv_abi.rs`
  - wired `/etc/hosts` subset resolution in ABI `getaddrinfo` for non-numeric names
- `crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs`
  - conformance executor supports optional `hosts_content` input for `getaddrinfo`
- `tests/conformance/fixtures/resolver.json`
  - added `getaddrinfo_hosts_file_subset` fixture

Commits carrying this work:
- `2a6d8aa` feat(resolv): add /etc/hosts file-backend lookup for getaddrinfo
- `ebb96f5` test(conformance): add hosts-file getaddrinfo resolver fixture

Verification (offloaded using `rch`, remote worker `vmi1152480`):
1. `RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core getaddrinfo_with_hosts -- --nocapture`
   - Result: PASS (4 tests)
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_ipv4_lookup`
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_respects_family_filter`
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_case_insensitive_alias`
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_no_network_fallback`
2. `RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc_conformance execute_getaddrinfo_case_uses_hosts_subset_when_provided -- --nocapture`
   - Result: PASS (1 test)

Operational note: an initial `rch` run in the active shared workspace hit rsync churn due concurrent file activity by other agents and attempted local fallback; I stopped those runs and re-ran in an isolated temporary worktree to keep execution fully remote/offloaded.
", "created_at": "2026-02-15T19:02:16Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-4f09","title":"io_internal_abi calls libc::tmpfile and libc::fclose directly, violating replacement guard","description":"The `emit_foreign_adoption_via_host_tmpfile` helper in `io_internal_abi.rs` calls `libc::tmpfile()` and `libc::fclose()` directly. This violates the standalone/replacement mode constraints and causes the `check_replacement_guard.sh replacement` check to fail, breaking the build. The test helper should use the native equivalents or be whitelisted.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:24:26.481202683Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:45:39.246535435Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:45:39.244569423Z","close_reason":"Removed stale replacement-guard exception after io_internal_abi switched to stdio_abi tmpfile/fclose; guard and standalone parity pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id": "bd-nklt", "title": "D.2: (SUPERSEDED) — folded into parent bd-9dq5", "description": "## Goal
Implement gai_suspend() which waits for one or more async DNS requests to complete, with optional timeout.

## POSIX Signature
```c
int gai_suspend(const struct gaicb *const list[], int nitems, const struct timespec *timeout);
```

Returns:
- 0: at least one request completed
- EAI_AGAIN: timeout expired before any completion
- EAI_ALLDONE: all requests already completed
- EAI_SYSTEM: error (errno set)

## Design
1. Scan the request list — if any are already completed (gai_error != EAI_INPROGRESS), return 0 immediately
2. If all are completed, return EAI_ALLDONE
3. Otherwise, wait using a futex on a shared completion counter:
   - Each request increments a completion futex when it transitions out of IN_PROGRESS
   - gai_suspend does FUTEX_WAIT on the counter with timeout
   - On wake, re-scan the list for completed requests
4. If timeout is NULL, wait indefinitely (block until at least one completes)
5. If timeout expires, return EAI_AGAIN

## Implementation
File: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs
- Replace gai_suspend body
- Add ASYNC_DNS_COMPLETION_FUTEX: AtomicU32 (incremented on each completion)
- Use SYS_futex for timed wait

## Testing
- All requests already done: returns EAI_ALLDONE
- Empty list (nitems=0): returns EAI_ALLDONE
- Null list: returns EAI_SYSTEM with EINVAL
- Timeout with no completions: returns EAI_AGAIN (use short 1ms timeout)
- One request completes immediately: returns 0

## Acceptance Criteria
gai_suspend correctly waits and returns appropriate status codes. Timeout is respected. Tests pass.", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:19:58.266246587Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-03T02:04:22.166397683Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-03T01:59:38.578636240Z", "close_reason": "Superseded: Track D redesigned as synchronous-only. No gai_suspend futex wait needed — function returns immediately since no async requests exist.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["async", "resolv", "stub-elimination"]}
+{"id":"bd-nqoa","title":"Metamorphic proptests for pthread condvar contract state machine","description":"cond.rs currently has unit tests covering individual contract transitions but no property-based metamorphic tests. Add MRs analogous to bd-73gu's mutex coverage: (1) Signal/Broadcast never block, (2) Wait/TimedWait always block, (3) Destroyed absorbing state, (4) Uninitialized absorbing state, (5) Broadcast drains Waiting→Idle regardless of has_waiters, (6) blocks⇒errno==0 structural invariant. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/cond.rs #[cfg(test)] module. One MR per commit, mutation-validated.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T18:43:10.806078382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:43:16.902455593Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-nyn5","title":"Conformance: direct execute strlen_strict fixture cases","description":"Add direct frankenlibc_fixture_exec coverage for strlen_strict.json cases in the harness alongside existing isolated harness coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T07:59:18.329904699Z","created_by":"SilverHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-18T08:07:06.991785263Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T08:07:06.989295441Z","close_reason":"Added direct execute_fixture_case coverage for strlen_strict fixtures and verified with rch test/check/clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","string","testing"]}
 {"id": "bd-oai", "title": "Epic: Dual-Mode Semantics & Deterministic Replay", "description": "Dual-mode execution: Strict (default, ABI-compatible, no repair, <20ns) and Hardened (deterministic repair per policy tables, structured JSON evidence per repair, <200ns). Mode selected immutably at process startup for deterministic replay. RuntimeKernelSnapshot (154-field state capture). Evidence symbol records with RaptorQ-inspired redundancy.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Success Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.", "notes": "### Session 30: Epic closed — all children completed

All child beads closed:
- bd-oai.1: Dual-mode selection + strict/hardened execution paths ✅
- bd-oai.2: RuntimeKernelSnapshot (165+ fields) + deterministic replay ✅  
- bd-oai.3: Evidence symbol records + RaptorQ-inspired redundancy ✅

Verification:
- 16 E2E tests in runtime_math_dual_mode_e2e_test.rs (all pass)
- 33 evidence system tests (all pass)
- Deterministic replay produces identical decision streams and JSONL logs
- Branch-diversity enforcement: Healthy/NearViolation/Violation states verified
- Golden snapshot regression gate: JSON equality under deterministic inputs", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-02-13T17:58:48.791246202Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-12T05:13:40.030481360Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-12T05:13:32Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["determinism", "dual-mode", "epic", "frankenlibc", "replay"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-oai", "depends_on_id": "bd-32e", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1124, "issue_id": "bd-oai", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.", "created_at": "2026-02-13T22:25:03Z"}]}
 {"id": "bd-oai.1", "title": "Dual-Mode: Mode selection at process startup (immutable, deterministic)", "description": "Implement mode selection: Strict vs Hardened chosen at process startup (env var / config), immutable thereafter. Must guarantee deterministic replay: same mode + same inputs = same outputs. Mode state stored in thread-local for zero-cost access.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.", "notes": "### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
